pkg/datetime.purpose: Custom datetime/duration value types layered over the standard library, github.com/rickb777/period, and govalues/decimal. Provides DateTime (RFC9557-aware time.Time wrapper) and ISODuration with calendar-correct, no-overflow date arithmetic used across billing, subscription, and entitlement period math.

patterns.name: DateTime wraps time.Time, never replaces it
patterns.description: DateTime embeds time.Time so all stdlib methods pass through; only Format/Parse/Add behavior is overridden. Construct with NewDateTime(t) and unwrap with AsTime().
patterns.example: type DateTime struct { time.Time }; func (t DateTime) AsTime() time.Time { return t.Time }

patterns.name: No-overflow calendar arithmetic via shiftClockTo
patterns.description: AddYearsNoOverflow/AddMonthsNoOverflow clamp to the last valid day of the target month (Jan 31 + 1M = Feb 28), and all Add* helpers route through shiftClockTo to mimic time.Add wall-clock behavior across DST.

patterns.name: ISODuration wraps period.Period
patterns.description: ISODuration embeds period.Period; build with NewISODuration(y,m,w,d,h,min,s) and round-trip strings through ISODurationString. Arithmetic (Add/Subtract/Mul) returns wrapped errors via NewDurationArithmeticError.

patterns.name: String types for ISO8601 wire form
patterns.description: ISODurationString is the serialized form; use .Parse()/.ParsePtrOrNil() to get an ISODuration and .ISOString()/.ISOStringPtrOrNil() to go back. Nil-safe pointer variants exist for optional fields.

patterns.name: Multi-format Parse with RFC9557 bracket timezone
patterns.description: Parse() accepts RFC3339, ISO8601 (incl. Zulu/fractional), and RFC9557 'ts[Area/City]' forms; it strictly rejects malformed brackets (nested, trailing text, empty tz). DateTime.UnmarshalJSON delegates to Parse.

patterns.name: Errors are constructors in errors.go
patterns.description: All error values come from New*Error helpers (NewDateTimeParseError, NewDurationParseError, NewDurationArithmeticError, NewInvalidTimezoneError) wrapping the underlying cause with %w; do not inline fmt.Errorf for these cases.

key_file_guides.role: DateTime type + Add(ISODuration) and the no-overflow Add* arithmetic helpers
key_file_guides.watch_for: MarshalJSON emits RFC3339 only (drops bracket tz and sub-second precision); shiftClockTo is the load-bearing helper — bypassing it breaks DST correctness

key_file_guides.role: ISODuration type, arithmetic, DivisibleBy, AddTo, convertPeriodToSeconds
key_file_guides.watch_for: DivisibleBy brute-forces 28/29/30/31-day months x 23/24/25-hour days, so 'P1Y divisible by PT8H' is intentionally false; AddTo always returns precise=true (kept for back-compat)
Strict bracket validation — any text after ']' or empty/nested brackets is an error; timezone is loaded via time.LoadLocation so unknown zones fail
layoutTZName is the literal 'Europe/Budapest' placeholder swapped out during Format; do not treat it as a default timezone
Falls back to ISO8601 layouts (fallbackRFC9557FormatLayout) when location string is empty

key_file_guides.role: ISODurationString parse/serialize helpers incl. nil-safe pointer variants
key_file_guides.watch_for: Parse() wraps failures in NewDurationParseError, not the raw period error
Use these instead of re-constructing NewISODuration for unit durations
Non-_test file but test-only; takes *testing.T and calls t.Fatalf

anti_patterns: Doing calendar math with time.AddDate directly instead of DateTime.Add* — reintroduces month-end overflow bugs the No-overflow helpers exist to prevent
Constructing time.Time/period.Period values directly and bypassing shiftClockTo, losing DST wall-clock semantics
Relying on DateTime.MarshalJSON to preserve bracket timezone or nanosecond precision (it serializes RFC3339)
Inlining fmt.Errorf for parse/arithmetic failures instead of the New*Error constructors
Treating layoutTZName ('Europe/Budapest') as a real default timezone

decisions.decision: Wrap rickb777/period and govalues/decimal rather than use time.Duration
decisions.rationale: time.Duration cannot represent calendar units (months/years) needed for billing periods, and decimal seconds avoid float drift

decisions.decision: DivisibleBy tests every realistic month/day length combination
decisions.rationale: Captures DST and variable-month-length edge cases so subscription cadence validation is conservative and correct

code_examples.scenario: Parse an ISO8601 duration and add it to a timestamp with no-overflow calendar math
code_examples.code:
import "github.com/openmeterio/openmeter/pkg/datetime"

d, err := datetime.ISODurationString("P1M").Parse()
if err != nil { return err }
next := datetime.NewDateTime(start).Add(d).AsTime() // 2024-01-31 + P1M = 2024-02-29

pkg/defaultx.purpose: Tiny generic helpers for defaulting optional values: WithDefault dereferences a pointer or returns a fallback, IfZero substitutes a fallback for the zero value. Used mainly in httpdriver/service layers to normalize optional API inputs.

patterns.name: Pointer-or-default for optional fields
patterns.description: WithDefault[T any](value *T, def T) T returns *value when non-nil, else def. Use for optional API request pointers.

patterns.name: Zero-or-default for comparable values
patterns.description: IfZero[T comparable](val, def T) T returns def when val equals the zero value. Use for non-pointer optionals like empty strings.

key_file_guides.watch_for: IfZero requires T comparable; cannot be used on slices/maps/funcs

anti_patterns: Adding non-trivial logic here — this package is intentionally two stateless generic functions

pkg/entitydiff.purpose: Generic three-way diff (Create/Update/Delete) of entity slices keyed by ID, with soft-delete awareness. Backs billing adapter reconciliation of persisted-vs-expected state for entities with and without child entities.

patterns.name: Entity interface contract
patterns.description: Diffed types must implement Entity (GetID() string, IsDeleted() bool). Empty GetID() means a not-yet-persisted item; IsDeleted() drives delete vs skip decisions.
patterns.example: type Entity interface { GetID() string; IsDeleted() bool }

patterns.name: DiffByID for entities with children
patterns.description: DiffByID(DiffByIDInput[T]) compares only by ID (never field equality) and dispatches HandleCreate/HandleUpdate/HandleDelete callbacks, joining their errors with errors.Join. Use when entities own children that need recursive handling.

patterns.name: DiffByIDEqualer for leaf entities
patterns.description: DiffByIDEqualer[T EqualerEntity[T]](expected, db) returns a Diff[T] and only marks an update when PersistedState.Equal(ExpectedState) is false. Use for entities with no children.

patterns.name: Soft-delete-aware correlation
patterns.description: diffByID treats IsDeleted()+no-DB-row as skip, expected-deleted+live-DB-row as Delete (carrying the expected/target state so co-edited fields persist), and DB rows absent from expected as Delete.

patterns.name: NestedEntity wrappers carry a parent
patterns.description: NestedEntity/EqualerNestedEntity wrap a child with its Parent while delegating GetID/IsDeleted/Equal to the child; build with NewEqualersWithParent to diff children while retaining parent context.

key_file_guides.role: Diff/DiffUpdate types, diffByID core, DiffByID + DiffByIDEqualer public entry points, Union
key_file_guides.watch_for: The internal diffByID returns UpdateCandidates (unfiltered); only DiffByIDEqualer applies Equal() to drop no-op updates — DiffByID emits every candidate as an update
Equal on the wrapper compares only the embedded Entity, not the Parent

anti_patterns: Using DiffByIDEqualer for entities with child entities — field equality skips updates whose only change is in children
Assuming an empty GetID() item will be correlated to a DB row — it is always treated as Create (or skipped if deleted)
Expecting DiffByID to compare fields; it correlates by ID only and relies on caller callbacks

decisions.decision: Split DiffByID (callback, children) from DiffByIDEqualer (Equal-based, leaf)
decisions.rationale: Parent entities need recursive child handling that field equality cannot express, while leaf entities benefit from automatic no-op-update suppression

decisions.decision: Delete entries carry the expected/target state, not the DB state, for expected-deleted rows
decisions.rationale: Co-edited fields (e.g. managedBy flipping to manual alongside deleted_at) must be persisted in the same change

code_examples.scenario: Reconcile leaf entities, applying only real updates

pkg/equal.purpose: Defines the Equaler[T] interface and nil-safe pointer comparison helpers, extracted from pkg/models specifically to avoid an import cycle. Backs equality-based diffing and value comparison across domain types.

patterns.name: Equaler interface duplicated to break a cycle
patterns.description: Equaler[T any] { Equal(other T) bool } lives here (not only in pkg/models) so low-level packages like pkg/entitydiff can depend on it without importing models. Implement Equal on domain types to satisfy it.
patterns.example: type Equaler[T any] interface { Equal(other T) bool }

patterns.name: Nil-safe pointer equality helpers
patterns.description: PtrEqual compares two *T where T is Equaler (both nil equal, one nil unequal); HasherPtrEqual does the same via hasher.Hasher.Hash().

key_file_guides.watch_for: Keep this package dependency-light (only pkg/hasher) — adding heavier imports risks recreating the models import cycle it exists to avoid

anti_patterns: Importing pkg/models from here — defeats the cycle-breaking purpose of duplicating Equaler
Adding domain-specific equality logic; this package holds only the generic interface and pointer helpers

decisions.decision: Duplicate Equaler from pkg/models instead of sharing
decisions.rationale: Documented in code: avoids a circular dependency between models and packages that need the interface

pkg/errorsx.purpose: Cross-cutting error utilities for the HTTP/server layer: prefix annotation that preserves errors.Join trees, a warn-level error wrapper, generated-API-error detection, and slog-based error Handlers implementing httptransport.ErrorHandler.

patterns.name: WithPrefix preserves joined-error structure
patterns.description: WithPrefix recursively prefixes each branch of an errors.Join tree (only at the top level via the Unwrap() []error interface) instead of flattening, so multi-error messages keep per-error prefixes.

patterns.name: warnError downgrades log severity
patterns.description: Wrap an error with NewWarnError to make SlogHandler log it at Warn instead of Error; detected via lo.ErrorsAs[*warnError]. context.Canceled and generated API errors are also logged as warnings.

patterns.name: Handler interface mirrors httptransport.ErrorHandler
patterns.description: Handler { Handle(err); HandleContext(ctx, err) }; SlogHandler (requires injected *slog.Logger) and NopHandler are the impls. Compile-time assertions enforce the interface match.

patterns.name: Generated API errors identified by package path
patterns.description: isAPIError reflects on the error type and walks the unwrap chain (single and []error) comparing PkgPath against api and api/v3 packages, because codegen emits no common base error type.

key_file_guides.watch_for: WithPrefix only recurses when the error implements Unwrap() []error at the top level (deliberate) — wrapping a join with fmt.Errorf hides the inner errors from per-branch prefixing
SlogHandler requires an injected logger (no slog.Default fallback per project convention); severity downgrade order is context.Canceled, then isAPIError, then warnError, else Error
Detection is by reflect PkgPath, so it breaks if generated error types move packages; both api and api/v3 must be listed
Encodes the non-top-level flattening behavior as expected output

key_file_guides.role: isAPIError detection tests for legacy/v3/wrapped/joined errors
key_file_guides.watch_for: Asserts wrapped and joined API errors are still detected

anti_patterns: Using slog.Default() to build SlogHandler — the logger must be injected explicitly per project convention
Flattening errors.Join before WithPrefix (e.g. via fmt.Errorf %w) — loses per-error prefixing
Hardcoding new API error type detection instead of extending apiErrorPackages with the generating package's path

decisions.decision: Detect generated API errors by reflecting on package path
decisions.rationale: oapi-codegen output provides no shared base error or marker interface, so package matching is the most maintainable signal

decisions.decision: Provide a warn-level error wrapper and treat API/cancellation errors as warnings
decisions.rationale: Keeps expected client-side and lifecycle errors out of error-level logs and alerting

code_examples.scenario: Build a server error handler that logs API/cancellation/warn errors at Warn level
